title: Notifying Social Security After a Death
summary: Social Security needs to be notified promptly, and the process is more straightforward than many families expect.
category: After a Loss II: Notifications & Accounts
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Notifying Social Security After a Death | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Social Security needs to be notified promptly, and the process is more straightforward than many families expect.
whatYouNeedToKnow: The funeral home often reports the death to Social Security automatically.
A family member can also report it directly by phone.
Any payment received for the month of death may need to be returned.

paragraphs: In many cases, the funeral home reports the death to Social Security as part of their standard process, but it's worth confirming this happened rather than assuming — a family member can also call the Social Security Administration directly to report it.

Social Security benefits are not payable for the month of death, so if a payment was received covering that month, it typically needs to be returned — the agency can explain the specific process for this.

nextSteps: Confirm with the funeral home whether they reported the death.
Call Social Security directly to confirm or report if needed.
Ask specifically about any payment that may need to be returned.
disclaimer: General information — the Social Security Administration can address the specific details of your situation.
